answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed.**

No — it contains two claims that are directly contradicted by the code, one unsubstantiated statistic, and a dishonest caption. Must be fixed or removed before sending:

- **"Export your visit history … Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." — FALSE.** `app/routers/visits.py` has only a `list_visits` endpoint that returns `[]`, followed by the comment `# TODO: pdf export`. The feature does not exist. This whole section (and its image reference) must be removed.
- **"Stronger account security … Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." — FALSE.** `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." The doc claims the exact opposite of the shipped default. Must be removed or rewritten to "available, opt-in per clinic."
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows." — Unsubstantiated.** There is nothing anywhere in the repository supporting this number. A hard statistic in a customer email needs a source; I would not stand behind it. Remove unless a source is provided.
- **"A refreshed scheduling calendar … shown with production data" — caption is false.** `captures.json` records both scheduling captures with `"state": "seed-demo"`, not production data (and see Q2 — the "after" isn't the calendar at all). Remove the "shown with production data" claim.
- **SMS reminders — plausibly accurate.** `app/notifications/sms.py` implements `SmsSender.send_reminder(...)` returning `{"channel": "sms"}`, so the feature code exists. Caveat: the only test (`tests/test_notifications.py`) is `assert True`, so nothing actually exercises it — but the claim itself is supported by code.

**2. The before/after scheduling comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. The "after" image is not the scheduling calendar. `captures.json` maps `after_scheduling.png` to `"route": "/billing/invoices"`, and opening the file confirms it renders **"BILLING / INVOICES"**, while `before_scheduling.png` renders **"SCHEDULING CALENDAR"**. A before/after pair that claims to show the redesigned calendar but whose "after" is the billing/invoices screen is exactly the "after that's actually an unrelated screen" failure. The pair must be recaptured against `/schedule` before this section can ship.

**3. Reconcile referenced images against files that exist.**

Three mismatches:

- **Referenced but missing:** the doc references `img/after_export.png` (line 26), but no such file exists in `docs/release/img/`. `captures.json` even flags it: `"expected capture; no corresponding file"`. This is a broken image in the email. (Moot if the export section is removed per Q1.)
- **Exists but unreferenced / mislabeled:** `after_scheduling.png` exists and is referenced, but its content is the billing/invoices page, not scheduling (Q2).
- **Orphan file:** `unused_capture_07.png` exists in the img folder but is referenced nowhere in the doc and appears nowhere in `captures.json`. Opening it shows **"PATIENT LIST"** — so it is not a usable substitute for the missing scheduling "after" either.

**4. Anything else that should not go to customers.**

- **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33).** This is internal-process/AI-attribution metadata and it labels the piece a "draft." Neither belongs in a finished customer email; strip it (per the owner's attribution policy — none is stated in this repo, so this should be confirmed with the owner rather than silently kept).
- **"shown with production data" (line 16-17).** Beyond being false (it's seed-demo), advertising that screenshots in a mass customer email contain real production data would be a patient-privacy red flag if it were true. The phrase should not appear regardless.
report:
I now have all the evidence I need. Here is my report.

## Plan

A draft "What's new in v4" customer email (`clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`) is about to go out. I set out to verify every factual and visual claim in it against the actual state of the `clinic-portal` repository — the feature code, the security defaults, the capture manifest, and the image files themselves — before it reaches clinic customers. My approach followed the docs-of-record skill: verify each claim against the actual code (the source of truth), run the screenshot-integrity check (every referenced image exists, every file is referenced, and every before/after pair shows the same surface), and check attribution/caption honesty for a customer-facing deliverable.

## Actions

- Read `.claude/skills/docs-of-record-and-arbitration/SKILL.md` — this guide shaped the whole approach: arbitrate doc-vs-code disputes in favor of code, run the screenshot integrity check (referenced-exists / captured-referenced / before-after-same-surface), enforce caption honesty, and obey the owner's attribution policy. It is the only reference document I consulted.
- `ls` of repo root and `find` for `docs/release` — located the doc and images under `clinic-portal/`, the only app with a `docs/release/` folder.
- Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` — the draft, with its five claims and six image references.
- Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json` — the capture manifest mapping each image to a route/state.
- `git log` / `git branch` in clinic-portal — single commit `24d8f6b "draft v4 release highlights with screenshots"`, one branch `main`. No decisions ledger or spec present.
- `find app tests docs` and read `app/routers/visits.py`, `app/security/config.py`, `app/notifications/email.py`, `app/notifications/sms.py`, `tests/test_notifications.py` — the actual feature code.
- Opened the image files: `before_scheduling.png` ("SCHEDULING CALENDAR"), `after_scheduling.png` ("BILLING / INVOICES"), `before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` (both "REMINDER SETTINGS"), `unused_capture_07.png` ("PATIENT LIST").

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed.**

No — it contains two claims that are directly contradicted by the code, one unsubstantiated statistic, and a dishonest caption. Must be fixed or removed before sending:

- **"Export your visit history … Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." — FALSE.** `app/routers/visits.py` has only a `list_visits` endpoint that returns `[]`, followed by the comment `# TODO: pdf export`. The feature does not exist. This whole section (and its image reference) must be removed.
- **"Stronger account security … Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." — FALSE.** `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." The doc claims the exact opposite of the shipped default. Must be removed or rewritten to "available, opt-in per clinic."
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows." — Unsubstantiated.** There is nothing anywhere in the repository supporting this number. A hard statistic in a customer email needs a source; I would not stand behind it. Remove unless a source is provided.
- **"A refreshed scheduling calendar … shown with production data" — caption is false.** `captures.json` records both scheduling captures with `"state": "seed-demo"`, not production data (and see Q2 — the "after" isn't the calendar at all). Remove the "shown with production data" claim.
- **SMS reminders — plausibly accurate.** `app/notifications/sms.py` implements `SmsSender.send_reminder(...)` returning `{"channel": "sms"}`, so the feature code exists. Caveat: the only test (`tests/test_notifications.py`) is `assert True`, so nothing actually exercises it — but the claim itself is supported by code.

**2. The before/after scheduling comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. The "after" image is not the scheduling calendar. `captures.json` maps `after_scheduling.png` to `"route": "/billing/invoices"`, and opening the file confirms it renders **"BILLING / INVOICES"**, while `before_scheduling.png` renders **"SCHEDULING CALENDAR"**. A before/after pair that claims to show the redesigned calendar but whose "after" is the billing/invoices screen is exactly the "after that's actually an unrelated screen" failure. The pair must be recaptured against `/schedule` before this section can ship.

**3. Reconcile referenced images against files that exist.**

Three mismatches:

- **Referenced but missing:** the doc references `img/after_export.png` (line 26), but no such file exists in `docs/release/img/`. `captures.json` even flags it: `"expected capture; no corresponding file"`. This is a broken image in the email. (Moot if the export section is removed per Q1.)
- **Exists but unreferenced / mislabeled:** `after_scheduling.png` exists and is referenced, but its content is the billing/invoices page, not scheduling (Q2).
- **Orphan file:** `unused_capture_07.png` exists in the img folder but is referenced nowhere in the doc and appears nowhere in `captures.json`. Opening it shows **"PATIENT LIST"** — so it is not a usable substitute for the missing scheduling "after" either.

**4. Anything else that should not go to customers.**

- **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33).** This is internal-process/AI-attribution metadata and it labels the piece a "draft." Neither belongs in a finished customer email; strip it (per the owner's attribution policy — none is stated in this repo, so this should be confirmed with the owner rather than silently kept).
- **"shown with production data" (line 16-17).** Beyond being false (it's seed-demo), advertising that screenshots in a mass customer email contain real production data would be a patient-privacy red flag if it were true. The phrase should not appear regardless.

## Next steps

If allowed to change files, I would: remove the PDF-export section and its image reference; remove/rewrite the 2FA section to "opt-in per clinic" to match `config.py` (or escalate to product on whether the default is actually changing); drop the "40% fewer no-shows" stat pending a cited source; recapture the scheduling "after" against `/schedule` and fix the `captures.json` entry (currently `/billing/invoices`); delete or correct the "shown with production data" caption; remove the AI-assistance/draft footer; and delete the orphan `unused_capture_07.png` or wire it in if intended. I would also add a real notification test instead of the `assert True` placeholder.

## Confidence and gaps

- High confidence on the 2FA and PDF-export inaccuracies (direct code contradiction), on the scheduling before/after being wrong, and on the image reconciliation — all verified by reading the code, the manifest, and the image contents directly.
- High confidence the "40% fewer no-shows" figure is unsupported *within this repo*; I cannot rule out an external source the team holds outside the repository.
- The images are rendered text placeholders (route labels), so I verified *which surface* each shows, not pixel-level UI fidelity. Confidence on the SMS claim is code-level only — the feature is coded but effectively untested. I found no decisions ledger or product spec in the repo, so arbitration rested on the code as source of truth.
